assertions.description: SpeechRecognition or webkitSpeechRecognition exists in window.
SpeechRecognition.available() is exposed as the static capability query for local speech packs.
assertions.test: (() => { const SR = window.SpeechRecognition || window.webkitSpeechRecognition; return !!SR && typeof SR.available === 'function'; })()

assertions.description: SpeechRecognition.install() is exposed for downloading local packs using the same options dictionary.
assertions.test: (() => { const SR = window.SpeechRecognition || window.webkitSpeechRecognition; return !!SR && typeof SR.install === 'function'; })()

assertions.description: SpeechRecognition exposes processLocally so recognition can be constrained to on-device processing.
assertions.test: (() => { const SR = window.SpeechRecognition || window.webkitSpeechRecognition; if (!SR) return false; try { return 'processLocally' in new SR(); } catch (_) { return false; } })()

assertions.description: SpeechRecognition.available() accepts quality values command, dictation, and conversation and returns an AvailabilityStatus.
assertions.test: (async () => { const SR = window.SpeechRecognition || window.webkitSpeechRecognition; if (!SR || typeof SR.available !== 'function') return false; const valid = new Set(['available', 'downloadable', 'downloading', 'unavailable']); for (const quality of ['command', 'dictation', 'conversation']) { const status = await SR.available({ langs: ['en-US'], processLocally: true, quality }); if (!valid.has(String(status))) return false; } return true; })()

assertions.description: The quality option is the SpeechRecognitionQuality enum, so an invalid tier is rejected.
assertions.test: (async () => { const SR = window.SpeechRecognition || window.webkitSpeechRecognition; if (!SR || typeof SR.available !== 'function') return false; try { await SR.available({ langs: ['en-US'], processLocally: true, quality: 'podcast' }); return false; } catch (error) { return error && error.name === 'TypeError'; } })()
